items.title: 2026/578: Methodist Church, Hurstlands, Hurst Green, Oxted, Surrey, RH8 0HG
items.description: Demolition of deconsecrated church building. Redevelopment of former methodist church site to provide a four-storey block of sixteen flats, comprising six one-bedroom flats, eight two-bedroom flats and two three-bedroom flats
items.headline: Plans Submitted to Replace Hurst Green Church with Flats
items.summary: A planning application proposes demolishing the deconsecrated Methodist Church on Hurstlands in Hurst Green. In its place would be a four-storey block of sixteen flats, including six one-bedroom, eight two-bedroom and two three-bedroom homes.

data.address: Methodist Church, Hurstlands, Hurst Green, Oxted, Surrey, RH8 0HG

items.title: 2026/790/TPO: 140 Bluehouse Lane, Limpsfield, Oxted, Surrey, RH8 0AR
items.description:
Please refer to photos provided:
T2) - Yew overhanging driveway - Laterals encroaching on drive to be crown lifted to 3m from ground level and reduced back from drive and garage roof by up to 2m.
T3) - Macrocarpa within neighbouring property overhanging right-hand boundary of front garden - To be cut back from garage and house creating at least 2m clearance.
items.headline: Tree work proposed at Bluehouse Lane home in Limpsfield
items.summary: A planning application asks to prune two protected trees at 140 Bluehouse Lane, Limpsfield. The plans cover crown lifting and cutting back a yew over the driveway, and trimming a neighbouring macrocarpa away from the garage and house.

data.address: 140 Bluehouse Lane, Limpsfield, Oxted, Surrey, RH8 0AR

items.description:
Please refer to photos provided: 
T1) - Red Oak on right-hand side of front garden - To crown reduce to previous points, removing up to 2m growth to a residual height of 14m and crown spread of 11m. Crown lifted to 5m from ground level over road and 2.5m over garden / driveway. Removing limbs <10cm dia. Repeat pruning for good arboricultural management.

items.summary: A tree work application has been submitted for a red oak in the front garden of 8 Farley Park, Oxted. The plans propose a crown reduction of up to 2m and crown lifting over the road and driveway, as part of routine tree care.
